source_jobs.title: Software Engineer, Product (Full Stack)
source_jobs.description:
COME BUILD THE RAILS OF B2B COMMERCE IN HOSPITALITY, STARTING WITH HOTELS




WHAT EXCITES YOU

 - You’re someone who has worked on early-stage teams, perhaps even as a founder, and love wearing multiple hats (to a healthy extent).

 - You've seen products come to life from just a spark of an idea, and even if things don't quite pan out, you're motivated by the process of making something from nothing.

 - You want to work on software that has real, tangible value to deliver to the people who need it, even if the solution can be basic or 'unsexy' sometimes.

 - You're an expert at prioritization and time management who can gracefully adapt to new facts on the fly as you make tradeoffs between customer and business needs.

 - You're focused on quality, not quantity. You can distill complex systems in the service of building delightful yet powerful applications.




WHAT EXCITES US

 - You have 8+ years of hands-on experience as a senior contributor to teams that have proved they can ship great products.

 - You're a full-stack generalist equally adept with with popular client-side and server-side technologies (Ruby on Rails / Hotwire / Typescript / React / Node.js / Postgres).

 - You have strong experience building responsive, intuitive user interfaces and can seamless transition between frontend and backend work.

 - You have solid object-oriented design and programming skills with experience architecting solutions for complex data-intensive systems.

 - You have experience building software that enables money movement with a penchant for digitizing historically paper-based processes.

 - You try to give back to the development community as a contributor to Open Source Software projects or other pro-bono avenues.




WORKING AT FOLIO

 - Folio is a Ruby on Rails app with Typescript Node.js automations deployed to AWS.

 - Our engineering team meets daily for standup (except Friday) and otherwise keep meetings to a minimum.

 - We're a remote-first company. Quarterly, we travel to work together in-person and stay with one of our hotel partners.

 - For the most part, we rely on Google Meet, GitHub, Linear, Notion, and Slack to make decisions and get things done day-to-day.




WHAT WE VALUE

 - Craft. We take pride in our work, find joy in execution, and believe that the best companies are built at a steady yet resilient pace.

 - Optimism. We believe in ourselves and each other and know anything is possible with the right amount of hustle and grit.

 - Ownership. We love making things better and have a strong sense of shared responsibility over everything we create together.

 - Hospitality. The industry we’re building for has a culture of service to others, and we bring that energy to how we work together each day.

source_jobs.title: Software Engineer, Backend
source_jobs.description:

WHAT EXCITES YOU

 - You’re someone who has worked on early-stage teams, perhaps even as a founder, and love wearing multiple hats (to a healthy extent).

 - You've seen products come to life from just a spark of an idea, and even if things don't quite pan out, you're motivated by the process of making something from nothing.

source_jobs.title: Sales
source_jobs.description:
SHAPE THE FUTURE OF HOSPITALITY COMMERCE—DRIVING SALES WITH LEADING HOTELS AND PROPERTY COMPANIES




WHAT EXCITES YOU

 - High ownership, high-accountability. Getting open contracts over the finish line

 - Using your 5+ years in hospitality sales or hotel tech sales.

 - Building lasting relationships and keeping the pipeline strong and moving

 - You’re passionate about travel, hospitality, and helping hotel teams thrive

 - You get energy from hosting demos, tailoring solutions, and navigating complex buying cycles with ease

 - You communicate clearly, build trust easily, and enjoy solving problems for customers

 - You’re organized, self-motivated, and thrive in fast-paced, high-ownership environments

 - You’ve used HubSpot or a similar CRM to manage pipelines and track activity

 - Bonus! You’ve sold to hotel management companies, or you know your way around hospitality procurement or hotel finance




WHAT EXCITES US

 - You’ve led the full sales cycle; sourcing leads, running demos, managing trials, and closing deals

 - You know how to build a healthy pipeline using a mix of outbound hustle, inbound follow-up, and long-standing industry relationships

 - You love crafting thoughtful, consultative demos that show hotel teams exactly how a product will solve their problems

 - You’ve partnered closely with Customer Success or Onboarding teams to make sure customers have a smooth experience post-sale

 - You get energy from meeting hotel teams in person—on-site, at conferences, or wherever real conversations happen

 - You’re always listening for feedback from the field and enjoy turning those insights into action; whether it’s product improvements or sharper messaging

 - You bring a strong network of hotel contacts




WORKING AT FOLIO

 - We're a remote-first company. Each quarter, we travel to work together in person and stay with one of our hotel partners.

 - Day to day, we collaborate using Hubspot, Google Meet, Linear, Notion, and Slack to make decisions and move quickly as a team.

 - You’ll have the opportunity to travel frequently to meet customers face-to-face and represent Folio in the field.

 - We’re a small team that works collaboratively and meets daily for standup.

 - We offer competitive compensation, equity, health benefits, flexible PTO, and a remote-first culture.

 - We value transparency, humility, and getting things done, and we’re building a team where people can do their best work and grow quickly.




WHAT WE VALUE

 - Craft. We take pride in our work, find joy in execution, and believe that the best companies are built at a steady yet resilient pace.

 - Optimism. We believe in ourselves and each other and know anything is possible with the right amount of hustle and grit.

 - Ownership. We love making things better and have a strong sense of shared responsibility over everything we create together.

 - Hospitality. The industry we’re building for has a culture of service to others, and we bring that energy to how we work together each day.

source_jobs.title: Software Engineer, Forward Deployed
source_jobs.description:
COME BUILD THE RAILS OF B2B COMMERCE IN HOSPITALITY, STARTING WITH HOTELS AND FINTECH INTEGRATIONS




WHAT EXCITES YOU

 - You thrive at the intersection of engineering and customer + product success, translating ambiguous business needs into elegant technical solutions that drive real value.

 - You're energized by working directly with customers to understand their challenges and architect solutions that transform how they operate.

 - You love owning the full customer journey - from initial discovery through production deployment and continuous iteration.

 - You're someone who has worked on early-stage teams, perhaps even as a founder, and excel at wearing multiple hats while maintaining focus on impact.

 - You're an expert at navigating ambiguity and making smart tradeoffs between customer needs, technical constraints, and business objectives.

 - You find satisfaction in building solutions that may not always be glamorous but deliver tangible, measurable value to the businesses that depend on them.




WHAT EXCITES US

 - You have 5+ years of hands-on experience shipping production systems, with a track record of driving customer outcomes from concept to deployment.

 - You're comfortable owning the customer journey from initial pre-sales discovery through production implementation and ongoing optimization.

 - You bring strong technical problem-solving skills, especially in fast-changing, ambiguous environments where requirements evolve.

 - You're a full-stack generalist equally adept with client-side and server-side technologies (Ruby on Rails / Hotwire / Typescript / React / Node.js / Postgres).

 - You have experience with system integrations, APIs, and building reliable data pipelines between enterprise systems.

 - You excel at technical communication - translating complex technical concepts for non-technical stakeholders and gathering clear requirements from vague business objectives.

 - You have experience building software for industries with complex operational workflows, ideally in hospitality, payments, or marketplace environments.

 - You understand that this is an engineering role with hands-on coding that also encompasses aspects of product management, technical customer success, and solution architecture.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Drive end-to-end implementations and solutions: Partner directly with our hotel partners and suppliers to architect, build, and deploy integrations with their existing ERP systems, property management systems, and operational tools.

 - Own the customer technical journey: Lead technical discovery sessions, design custom solutions, and ensure successful production deployments that meet specific business requirements. You’ll understand user pain points, scope product specs, and design and build software.

 - Build scalable integration frameworks: Create reusable patterns and self-serve tools that accelerate future implementations while maintaining flexibility for customer-specific needs.

 - Enable rapid onboarding: Contribute to our hotel onboarding initiative by identifying patterns across implementations and building features that reduce time-to-value for new partners.

 - Shape the product roadmap: Surface unmet needs from your direct customer work, prototype solutions, and collaborate with product and engineering to build features that benefit all customers.

 - Develop strategic partnerships with our sales and go-to-market-teams: Work closely with key technology partners to ensure deep, reliable integrations that can become distribution channels for Folio.
   
   


WORKING AT FOLIO

 - Folio is a Ruby on Rails app with Typescript Node.js automations deployed to AWS.

 - Our engineering team meets daily for standup (except Friday) and otherwise keep meetings to a minimum.

 - We're a remote-first company. Quarterly, we travel to work together in-person and stay with one of our hotel partners.

 - For the most part, we rely on Google Meet, GitHub, Linear, Notion, and Slack t

source_jobs.title: Customer Support
source_jobs.description:
Folio https://folio.co/ is a fast-growing software startup on a mission to improve hospitality profitability, starting with simplifying how hospitality businesses manage spend. Our financial operations platform transforms the procure-to-pay process, helping operators gain control and visibility over how money moves through their business. Our customers include over 300 hotels across industry leaders.

We recently closed our Series A co-led by Construct Capital and Thrive Capital.

We're looking for a Customer Support Specialist to deliver exceptional service to our hotel customers and partners. You'll be the friendly, reliable voice of Folio — helping customers navigate our platform, resolving issues quickly, and ensuring every interaction leaves them feeling supported. If you love helping people, take pride in delivering great service, and thrive in a fast-paced environment, this is the role for you.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Product Support: Serve as the first point of contact for customers, troubleshooting issues and escalating complex inquiries as needed. We utilize Intercom to manage our customer interactions via email and messages primarily.

 - Voice of the Customer: Collaborate with product, design and engineering to continuously improve our products and the customer experience, advocating for our customer needs and priorities. Partner with GTM to help our customers make the most out of Folio.

 - Operational Processes and Documentation: Handle operational requests and proactively drive improvements in our processes and internal workflows. Create documentation to help our customers solve their issues themselves and to level up our internal teams.

 - Build with us: Help shape the future of Folio Support, leveraging AI and world-class technologies as we scale. We are reimagining a seamless experience for our customers, bringing personalization, empathy and speed to the customer experience.

You'll have high visibility across the company and work directly with founders, engineering, sales and others. This is a great opportunity to have real ownership over the entire customer experience end to end as we scale.


WHAT EXCITES US

 - 3+ years of customer or technical support experience at a high-growth tech startup.

 - Intercom and support tools knowledge: You're highly proficient in Intercom and have experience optimizing for technology solutions to improve the customer and our teams experience (automations, AI driven self self service solutions, knowledge base, etc.)

 - Customer Success: You are passionate about helping customers, improving their experience and making them succeed.

 - Exceptional Communication: Excellent verbal and written communication skills with ability to simplify complex topics into concise messages across different audiences, and serve our customers with professionalism and empathy.

 - Impact and velocity: Experience handling competing priorities and demonstrated bias for action. You thrive in ambiguity and rapidly adapt to change.

 - Bonus: Experience in or passion for hotels, hospitality, or travel.


WHAT EXCITES YOU

 - Working in a fast-paced, high-accountability environment where your work directly impacts customers and the business.

 - Building processes from scratch and proposing innovative solutions to customer problems and internal operations. There are not old playbooks to follow!

 - Building strong relationships with customers, becoming their trusted partner and an advocate for their needs. Your feedback will drive tangible product improvements.

 - Working cross-functionally with a supportive team that values your input, and where you can expand your knowledge.

 - Joining a growing company early — with real opportunity to grow as we scale

 - Having ownership and autonomy over how you manage your work


WORKING AT FOLIO

We're a small but mighty team backed by top-tier investors and operators. Our customers include some of the most respected names in hospitality, and we're growing

source_jobs.title: Product Marketing Manager
source_jobs.description:
What excites you

 - You want to be early. You like being close to the product, the customer, and the messiness of figuring things out.

 - You enjoy turning sharp customer insight into positioning that actually changes behavior.

 - You care about story-telling. You want to build narratives from launches and words to be thoughtful and useful, not generic or AI slop.

 - You’re energized by hospitality. Hotels are complex, human, and operationally intense. That makes the work interesting, particularly in an age of AI.

 - You want real ownership. If something ships poorly, you fix it. If something lands well, you know exactly why.

What excites us

 - You’ve spent 5–7 years in product marketing at a high-growth company, ideally B2B SaaS or complex marketplaces.

 - You can explain technical or operational products in a way that feels simple and grounded. You’ve owned messaging and positioning end to end in close partnership with product, sales, and leadership. You’ve rewritten it when it didn’t work.

 - You’ve led go-to-market for real products, not just features. You know how to scope a launch, make tradeoffs, and ship. You care to measure impact post-launch.

 - You’re not precious about what “typical” PMMs look like. You like wearing different hats and can go beyond product marketing to engage contractors for events planning, or work with an agency to test some ads, when needed.

 - You’re comfortable going deep. Customer calls, sales calls, demos, win-loss. You look for signal, not anecdotes.

 - You’re hands-on. You write the first draft. You build the deck. You partner with design, but you don’t wait on them.

 - Bonus if you’ve worked in hospitality, travel, fintech, or vertical SaaS. Bigger bonus if you genuinely like hotels.

Working at Folio

 - We're a remote-first company. Three times a year, we travel to work together in-person and stay with one of our hotel partners.

 - We love experimenting with new tools. For the most part, we rely on Google Meet, Linear, Notion, and Slack to make decisions and get things done day-to-day.

 - You will have the opportunity to travel frequently and meet our customers in person at conferences and events, which you will be helping plan for.

 - We're a small team who work collaboratively and meet daily for stand up

What we value

 - Craft. We take pride in our work, find joy in execution, and believe that the best companies are built at a steady yet resilient pace.

 - Optimism. We believe in ourselves and each other and know anything is possible with the right amount of hustle and grit.

 - Ownership. We love making things better and have a strong sense of shared responsibility over everything we create together.

 - Hospitality. The industry we’re building for has a culture of service to others, and we bring that energy to how we work together each day.

source_jobs.title: Partnership Manager, AAHOA
source_jobs.description:
Folio is the official purchasing technology provider for the AAHOA Marketplace, serving 20,000+ hotel owners across the country. We're looking for a Partnerships Manager to be the face of Folio within the AAHOA ecosystem: owning the on-the-ground relationship, driving co-marketing execution, and helping grow marketplace adoption across the membership.

This is a high-visibility, high-autonomy role. You'll be traveling a significant amount, at AAHOA conferences, regional events, town halls, and golf tournaments building relationships with hotel owners, AAHOA leadership, and supplier partners. Between events, you'll coordinate co-branded campaigns, track & work on hitting adoption milestones, and work cross-functionally with our product and operations teams to support AAHOA member needs. You'll be measured on the health and growth of the AAHOA partnership -- member adoption, marketplace engagement, and the strength of the relationships you build across the ecosystem.

What excites you

 - Being the person who shows up, builds trust, and becomes a known name in a community. You thrive when you're the go-to contact for a major partner.

 - Traveling regularly to meet hotel owners and AAHOA stakeholders face-to-face at conferences, regional meetings, town halls, golf outings. You know nothing replaces being in the room.

 - Owning a partnership end-to-end: co-marketing execution, event logistics, member outreach, adoption tracking, and relationship management with senior stakeholders.
   Working with a small, fast-moving team where your work directly impacts the trajectory of a critical partnership.

 - Coordinating across internal teams (product, operations, leadership) and external stakeholders (AAHOA staff, GPOs, supplier contacts) to keep complex workstreams moving.

 - Working with Folio's leadership team to build a scalable partnerships structure and associated processes to enable the company to rapidly expand.

 - Having ownership and autonomy over how you manage your work.
   

What excites us

 - 5–8+ years of experience in partnerships, business development, account management, or a senior client-facing role, ideally in hospitality, procurement, or a high-growth startup environment.

 - You've managed complex, multi-stakeholder partnerships involving associations, GPOs, franchise networks, or membership organizations.

 - You have strong executive presence. You're comfortable presenting to a room of hotel owners, navigating a golf outing with AAHOA board members, or running a webinar for 200 attendees.

 - You're organized, detail-oriented, and thrive when you're juggling events, campaigns, and relationship touchpoints across a portfolio of stakeholders.

 - You're willing to travel 30–40%+ of the time, including to major hospitality conferences and AAHOA regional events across the US.



Bonus points for:

 - Gujarati language fluency (a significant portion of the AAHOA membership prefers to conduct business in Gujarati)

 - Experience in hospitality, hotel operations, or deep familiarity with the independent hotel ownership community

 - Existing relationships within the AAHOA network

 - Familiarity with GPOs or hospitality procurement



Working at Folio

 - We're a remote-first company. Quarterly, we travel to work together in-person and stay with one of our hotel partners.

 - For the most part, we rely on Google Meet, Linear, Notion, and Slack to make decisions and get things done day-to-day.

 - You will have the opportunity to travel frequently and meet our customers and partners in person.

 - We're a small team who work collaboratively and meet daily for stand up.



What we value

 - Craft. We take pride in our work, find joy in execution, and believe that the best companies are built at a steady yet resilient pace.

 - Optimism. We believe in ourselves and each other and know anything is possible with the right amount of hustle and grit.

 - Ownership. We love making things better and have a strong sense of s

source_jobs.title: Product Ops, Buy
source_jobs.description:
Folio Buy is the engine behind how we onboard new hotel customers, e.g. configuring storefronts, catalogs, and approval workflows. Today, this process involves manual coordination across internal teams, property teams, and suppliers. We're looking for a Folio Buy Product Ops lead to own this end-to-end workflow, identify bottlenecks, and drive improvements that dramatically accelerate setup effort spanning different functional work.

This is a technically-minded product operations role. You'll sit at the intersection of GTM, operations, and engineering to translate messy, real-world pain points into scoped product work, build programmatic monitoring and tooling to operationalize manual work, and define the path toward a simpler, faster, more self-serve setup experience.



What excites you

 - Taking a complex, multi-step operational workflow and making it dramatically faster and more reliable. You get satisfaction from shaving days off a process and eliminating manual steps.

 - Digging into the details of how things actually work today — shadowing implementation calls, reading support tickets, mapping out every step and handoff — so you can find the real bottlenecks.

 - Translating qualitative pain points ("this is frustrating," "we keep going back and forth") into clear, prioritized product requirements and experiments.

 - Working at the intersection of product, operations, and engineering — you're not a pure PM and you're not a pure ops person. You're comfortable doing both, and you're technical enough to build tooling and monitoring yourself.

 - Building processes, playbooks, and programmatic tooling from scratch, and iteratively improving existing workflows.

 - Working with a small, fast-moving team where your improvements directly impact customers and the business. Having ownership and autonomy over how you manage your work.



What you'll do

 - Own the Folio Buy workflow end-to-end. Map the current process, identify bottlenecks, and maintain a clear view of where time and effort are spent.

 - Prioritize and scope product improvements. Cut through the noise of pain points and feature requests to define P0/P1/P2 improvements to the setup experience, especially toward a simpler onboarding path.

 - Partner with operations & engineering. Work closely with the team doing setups day-to-day to close the loop on what's working, what's breaking, and where customers and suppliers get stuck.

 - Build programmatic monitoring and tooling. Write code (or vibe code) to operationalize manual work: automate repetitive steps, build monitoring dashboards, and create tools that give the team visibility into setup health.

 - Design and improve internal processes. Build better playbooks, templates, and admin workflows that reduce manual effort, from supplier account setup sheets to bulk import flows.

 - Support some business development work to enable improvements in the customer experience.



What excites us

 - 3–6 years of experience in product operations, product management, technical operations, or a product-adjacent role at a high-growth startup or SaaS company.

 - You're a technically sharp, product-oriented operator: analytical, dynamic, and comfortable writing code. You can map a messy process, identify the highest-leverage fix, and drive it to completion, including building the tooling yourself.

 - You have strong user experience and onboarding intuition — you know how to simplify flows, reduce friction, and shorten time-to-value.

 - You're comfortable with systems and tooling: designing internal processes, building playbooks, and improving admin tools that support operational workflows. You are excellent at scoping and prioritization

 - You communicate clearly and can translate between operational teams ("this is broken") and engineering ("I’ve scoped a potential solution that could reduce the time this step takes by Y days").

 - You love fast-paced environments; you're resourceful, proactive, and don't wait around

notes: Early-stage startup with a strong engineering focus, building a B2B commerce platform for hospitality.
